globalResearchNotes.cuba_cigb: Centro de Ingenieria Genetica y Biotecnologia (CIGB) in Havana has been the world leader in GHRP-6 cytoprotective research since early 2000s. Key investigators: Jorge Berlanga-Acosta, Diana Garcia del Barco, Gerardo Guillen-Nieto. Unique contributions include stroke combination therapy (EGF+GHRP-6), doxorubicin cardioprotection, liver fibrosis, and wound healing applications.
globalResearchNotes.japan: Japanese researchers (Kojima, Kangawa, Nakazato) made the landmark discovery of ghrelin in 1999 using GHRP-6 as a tool ligand. This 'reverse pharmacology' approach led to fundamental understanding of the GHS system. Ongoing ghrelin system research continues at National Cardiovascular Center and Kurume University.
globalResearchNotes.europe: Italian researchers (Ghigo, Arvat, Muller) at University of Turin contributed extensively to human pharmacodynamic characterization and selectivity profiling. German groups (Thevis, Thomas) established anti-doping detection methods. Spanish groups (Casanueva, Dieguez, Popovic) characterized hypothalamic mechanisms. Swedish researchers (Tivesten, Isgaard) contributed cardioprotection studies.
globalResearchNotes.usa_canada: US (Bowers, Thorner) developed GHRP-6 and conducted foundational studies. Canadian researchers identified CD36 as secondary receptor mediating GH-independent cardioprotection.
globalResearchNotes.china: Chinese researchers have contributed to analogue development and mechanism studies, with ongoing interest in metabolic applications.

notes: GHRP-6 is historically significant as the peptide that led to discovery of ghrelin and the GHS receptor system. Cuban CIGB has emerged as the global leader in cytoprotective research with unique clinical trial programs. The peptide has established human PK/PD data but no Phase 3 efficacy trials for GH-related endpoints. Development was discontinued in favor of more selective compounds like ipamorelin, but cytoprotective research continues actively.
